title: generate_fresh_address: Result<String, PaymentAction> uses Err as a business response
description: generate_fresh_address returns Result<String, PaymentAction>. The Err variant carries PaymentAction::Unknown{...} — not an error in the Rust sense (unexpected failure) but the actual response to send to the payer. The caller does: Err(unknown) => return send_payment_message(session_id, unknown, ...). Using Err to carry a business response inverts the idiomatic meaning of Result::Err. Suggested rewrite: return an enum or a newtype that makes the two paths explicit. For example: enum AddressResult { Address(String), SendUnknown(PaymentAction) }. Alternatively, return Ok(Either<String, PaymentAction>). The current pattern compiles and works, but any future contributor seeing Result<String, PaymentAction> will assume Err means 'something went wrong' and treat it with ? propagation, which would incorrectly silence the Unknown reply.
